description: Paracetamol is used to treat mild to moderate pain and reduce fever. It is generally safe for most people when taken at recommended doses, and side effects are rare.
mechanism: Reduces prostaglandin production in the brain, helping to lower pain and body temperature.

dosage.adults: 500mg to 1000mg every 4 to 6 hours as needed (maximum 4000mg per day)
dosage.children: Dose depends on age and weight; should follow medical advice or product instructions

side_effects: Liver damage in case of overdose
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dosage
Use with caution in liver disease
Overdose can cause serious liver damage
combination_note: May be combined with other medicines in cold and flu treatments or pain relief formulations.
embedding_text: Paracetamol is an analgesic and antipyretic drug used to treat pain such as headache, toothache, sprains, and fever from cold and flu. It works by reducing prostaglandin production in the brain. It is safe at recommended doses but overdose can cause liver damage.

description: Amoxicillin is a penicillin-type antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections in various parts of the body including the ear, nose, throat, skin, urinary tract, and respiratory system.
mechanism: Inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis, leading to the destruction of bacteria.

dosage.adults: Typically 250mg to 500mg every 8 hours or 500mg to 875mg every 12 hours depending on infection severity
dosage.children: Dose depends on body weight and type of infection; should follow medical guidance

onset: Begins working within a few hours of administration
duration: Usually prescribed for 5 to 14 days depending on infection
warnings: Do not use in patients with penicillin allergy
May cause severe allergic reactions including anaphylaxis
Risk of Clostridium difficile-associated diarrhea
Use only for bacterial infections to avoid resistance
combination_note: Often used in combination with other drugs for treating Helicobacter pylori infections.
embedding_text: Amoxicillin is a penicillin-class antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections such as ear, throat, respiratory, urinary, and skin infections. It works by inhibiting bacterial cell wall synthesis. It should not be used in patients allergic to penicillin and may cause gastrointestinal side effects or allergic reactions.

description: Azithromycin is a prescription antibiotic used to treat a wide range of bacterial infections affecting the respiratory system, skin, genitals, and other parts of the body.
mechanism: Inhibits bacterial protein synthesis by binding to the 50S ribosomal subunit, thereby stopping bacterial growth.

dosage.adults: Typically 250mg to 500mg once daily depending on infection; duration varies (often 3 to 5 days)
dosage.children: Dose depends on body weight and infection type; should follow medical guidance

onset: Begins working within a few hours, but full effect may take a few days
duration: Usually prescribed for short courses (3 to 5 days), but effects persist longer due to long half-life
warnings: Use with caution in patients with heart rhythm disorders (QT prolongation)
Caution in liver or kidney disease
May worsen myasthenia gravis
Risk of severe allergic reactions
Use carefully during pregnancy and breastfeeding under medical advice
contraindications: Allergy to azithromycin or other macrolide antibiotics

hiv_patients: Treatment of opportunistic infections such as MAC and pneumonia

combination_note: May be used in combination with other drugs for treating specific infections such as MAC or severe bacterial infections.
embedding_text: Azithromycin is a macrolide antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections such as pneumonia, sinusitis, bronchitis, skin infections, and sexually transmitted infections. It works by inhibiting bacterial protein synthesis. It is also used in HIV patients to treat and prevent opportunistic infections like Mycobacterium avium complex.

description: Atorvastatin is a lipid-lowering medication used to reduce cholesterol levels and decrease the risk of cardiovascular events such as heart attack and stroke.
mechanism: Inhibits HMG-CoA reductase enzyme in the liver, reducing cholesterol synthesis and increasing removal of LDL cholesterol from the bloodstream.

dosage.adults: Typically 10mg to 80mg once daily depending on condition and response
dosage.children: Dose depends on age and condition; should follow medical guidance

onset: Initial effects seen within a few days; full effect within 2 to 4 weeks
duration: Long-term use required for sustained cholesterol control
administration: Take once daily at the same time each day
Can be taken with or without food (tablet)
Oral suspension should be taken on an empty stomach
Do not exceed prescribed dosage
Continue even if feeling well unless advised otherwise
serious_side_effects: Muscle pain, tenderness, or weakness (possible myopathy)
warnings: Do not use in patients with active liver disease
Avoid excessive alcohol consumption
May increase risk of muscle-related disorders
Regular liver function monitoring required
Risk increases in elderly patients
embedding_text: Atorvastatin is a statin medication used to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart attack and stroke. It works by inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase, decreasing cholesterol production in the liver. It is used long-term along with diet and lifestyle changes and may cause muscle-related or liver-related side effects.

description: Cetirizine is an antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes, and hives by blocking histamine activity in the body.
mechanism: Blocks histamine H1 receptors, preventing allergic reactions and reducing symptoms such as itching, swelling, and redness.

dosage.children: Dose depends on age and weight; should follow medical guidance

administration: Take once daily at the same time
Can be taken with or without food
Orally disintegrating tablets should be handled with dry hands and allowed to dissolve on the tongue
serious_side_effects: Severe allergic reaction (rash, difficulty breathing, swelling)
warnings: May cause drowsiness; avoid driving until effects are known
Avoid alcohol as it increases sedation
Use with caution in kidney or liver disease
Do not use as a substitute for emergency treatment of severe allergic reactions (anaphylaxis)
contraindications: Allergy to cetirizine or similar antihistamines
special_instructions: Do not use for unusual hives (non-itchy, bruised, or blistered)
Seek medical help if hives persist beyond 6 weeks
Stop and consult doctor if no improvement within 3 days
embedding_text: Cetirizine is a second-generation antihistamine used to treat allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, itchy eyes, and hives. It works by blocking histamine receptors and provides relief for 24 hours. It may cause drowsiness and should not be used as a substitute for emergency treatment of severe allergic reactions.

mechanism_of_action: Inhibits production of prostaglandins, reducing pain, inflammation, and fever

dosage_instructions.general: Take exactly as prescribed by doctor
special_notes: Take at same time daily if regular use
Powder must be mixed with water before use

precautions: Allergy to NSAIDs (e.g., ibuprofen, naproxen)
Asthma or nasal polyps
Heart failure
Liver or kidney disease
Pregnancy (avoid after 20 weeks)
Elderly patients (75+)
Phenylketonuria (PKU)
missed_dose: Take as soon as remembered unless close to next dose; do not double dose
dietary_instructions: Continue normal diet unless advised otherwise
storage: Keep away from heat and moisture
Keep out of reach of children
disposal: Keep away from pets and children

description: Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used to reduce pain, inflammation, and fever by inhibiting prostaglandin production.
mechanism: Blocks cyclooxygenase (COX) enzymes, reducing prostaglandin synthesis responsible for pain, inflammation, and fever

dosage.adults: Typically every 4 to 6 hours as needed; do not exceed recommended daily limit
dosage.children: Dose based on age and weight; usually every 6 to 8 hours with strict limits

administration: Take with food or milk to reduce stomach irritation
Swallow tablets whole; do not crush or chew
Shake liquid forms before use
Use proper measuring device for liquid doses
Take at same time daily if prescribed regularly
serious_side_effects: Liver problems (yellowing of skin or eyes)
warnings: Avoid use during pregnancy after 20 weeks
Do not combine with other NSAIDs
May increase risk of gastrointestinal irritation or bleeding
Use cautiously in elderly patients
Not recommended for young children under 4 years in combination products
contraindications: History of severe allergic reaction to aspirin or similar drugs
special_instructions: Do not exceed maximum daily dose
Avoid duplicate use with combination cold medications
Stop use if symptoms worsen or persist beyond recommended duration
Seek medical advice if fever lasts more than 3 days or pain more than 10 days
embedding_text: Ibuprofen is a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. It is commonly used for headaches, arthritis, menstrual pain, and minor injuries. It works by blocking prostaglandin production and should be taken with food to minimize stomach irritation.

description: Loperamide is an antidiarrheal medication used to reduce the frequency of bowel movements by slowing intestinal movement and decreasing fluid loss.
uses: Chronic diarrhea associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)
Reduction of stool output in ileostomy patients
mechanism: Slows intestinal motility and reduces secretion of fluids and electrolytes into the bowel

dosage.adults: Taken after each loose stool; do not exceed maximum daily dose
dosage.children: Dose based on age/weight; not recommended for children under 2 years

administration: Take after each loose stool (for acute diarrhea)
Follow strict dosing limits
Use proper measuring device for liquid forms
Do not use household spoons for measurement
warnings: Do not use in children under 2 years
Avoid if diarrhea is accompanied by fever or bloody stools
Use cautiously in liver disease
May cause drowsiness—avoid driving until effects are known
Stop use if diarrhea lasts more than 48 hours
interactions: Drugs causing QT prolongation (risk of arrhythmia in overdose)
special_instructions: Stay hydrated with fluids and electrolytes
Stop use if symptoms worsen
Do not exceed recommended dose
Seek medical help for persistent diarrhea
embedding_text: Loperamide is an antidiarrheal medication used to control acute and chronic diarrhea. It works by slowing down intestinal movement and reducing fluid loss. It should be used carefully, avoided in cases of infection-related diarrhea, and stopped if symptoms persist beyond 48 hours.

description: Loratadine is a second-generation antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms by blocking histamine activity in the body.
mechanism: Blocks histamine H1 receptors, preventing allergic responses

dosage.children: Dose depends on age and formulation

administration: Take once daily with or without food
Do not exceed recommended dose
Place dissolving tablet on tongue without breaking
Swallow with or without water
warnings: Use cautiously in liver or kidney disease
May cause mild drowsiness in some individuals
Chewable tablets may contain aspartame (important for PKU patients)
contraindications: Known allergy to loratadine or its components
special_instructions: Take regularly for best allergy control
Avoid combining with similar allergy medications unless prescribed
Consult doctor if symptoms persist
embedding_text: Loratadine is an antihistamine used to treat allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. It works by blocking histamine and is typically taken once daily with minimal drowsiness compared to older antihistamines.

description: Metformin is an oral antidiabetic medication used to control blood sugar levels in people with type 2 diabetes by reducing glucose production and improving insulin sensitivity.
uses: Prevention of diabetes-related complications (heart, kidney, nerve, eye damage)
mechanism: Decreases hepatic glucose production, reduces intestinal glucose absorption, and increases insulin sensitivity

dosage.adults: Taken 1 to 3 times daily with meals; extended-release typically once daily
dosage.titration: Started at low dose and gradually increased every 1–2 weeks

administration: Take with meals to reduce gastrointestinal side effects
Swallow extended-release tablets whole
Take at the same time daily
Do not stop without medical advice
warnings: Not for type 1 diabetes
Monitor blood sugar regularly
Risk of vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use
Dose adjustments needed with lifestyle changes (diet/exercise)
Use cautiously in kidney or liver disease
interactions: Insulin and other antidiabetic drugs (risk of hypoglycemia)
special_instructions: Follow strict diet and exercise plan
Monitor blood glucose regularly
Continue even if feeling well
Consult doctor before stopping
Report unusual fatigue or breathing issues immediately
embedding_text: Metformin is a first-line oral medication for type 2 diabetes that lowers blood sugar by reducing glucose production in the liver and improving insulin sensitivity. It is taken with meals and is essential for long-term diabetes management and prevention of complications.

uses: Treatment and prevention of H. pylori-related ulcers
mechanism_of_action: Reduces stomach acid production by blocking proton pumps in the stomach lining

special_instructions: Swallow tablets/capsules whole
Do not crush or chew
Granules must be mixed with water before use
May be taken 2–3 times daily for certain conditions

warnings: Not for immediate heartburn relief
Long-term use may increase risk of fractures
May cause vitamin B12 deficiency
Consult doctor if symptoms persist beyond 14 days (OTC)
precautions: Inform doctor about allergies
Check for drug interactions (e.g., St. John's wort, iron)
Use caution in patients with osteoporosis or liver disease
Consult doctor if pregnant or breastfeeding
missed_dose: Take as soon as remembered unless close to next dose; do not double dose
storage: Keep away from heat, moisture, and light
Keep out of reach of children

uses: Prevention of nausea and vomiting caused by chemotherapy
Prevention of nausea and vomiting caused by radiation therapy
Prevention of nausea and vomiting after surgery
mechanism_of_action: Blocks serotonin (5-HT3) receptors to prevent nausea and vomiting

administration.frequency: 1 to 3 times daily during and after treatment as directed
special_instructions: Do not chew oral film
Use dry hands to handle disintegrating tablets
Place disintegrating tablet on tongue to dissolve

warnings: Avoid use with apomorphine
May cause heart rhythm problems (QT prolongation)
Use caution in patients with liver disease or electrolyte imbalance
Monitor for serotonin syndrome when combined with other medications
precautions: Inform doctor about allergies to similar medications
Avoid if taking apomorphine
Report history of heart rhythm disorders
Use caution if pregnant or breastfeeding
Patients with PKU should note presence of aspartame in some forms
missed_dose: Take as soon as remembered unless close to next dose; do not double dose
storage: Keep out of reach of children

uses: Healing of esophageal damage caused by acid reflux
Treatment of conditions with excess stomach acid (e.g., Zollinger-Ellison syndrome)
mechanism_of_action: Decreases stomach acid production by inhibiting proton pumps in the stomach lining

timing: Twice daily for high acid conditions
special_instructions: Swallow tablets whole; do not crush or chew
Granules must be mixed with applesauce or apple juice only
Do not mix granules with other liquids or foods
Consume granule mixture within 10 minutes

warnings: Long-term use may increase risk of bone fractures
May cause low magnesium, calcium, or vitamin B12 levels
Use caution in patients with osteoporosis or autoimmune diseases
Possible interaction with iron supplements
Use caution in elderly patients (70+ years)
precautions: Inform doctor about allergies to PPIs
Discuss all medications before starting
Monitor electrolyte levels if at risk
Consult doctor if pregnant or breastfeeding
missed_dose: Take as soon as remembered unless close to next dose; do not double dose
storage: Keep away from heat and moisture
Keep out of reach of children

uses: Management of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD)
mechanism_of_action: Relaxes airway smooth muscles by stimulating beta-2 receptors, leading to bronchodilation and improved airflow

special_instructions: Use inhaler with proper technique
Shake inhaler before use
Use spacer if recommended
Do not exceed prescribed doses

warnings: Overuse may lead to decreased effectiveness
May cause rapid heart rate or palpitations
Use cautiously in patients with heart disease, hypertension, or diabetes
Not a substitute for long-term asthma control medications
precautions: Inform doctor about heart conditions or high blood pressure
Use caution in patients with thyroid disorders or diabetes
Consult doctor if pregnant or breastfeeding
Monitor frequency of use (increased use may indicate worsening condition)
missed_dose: Use as needed; if on a schedule, take as soon as remembered unless close to next dose
storage: Keep inhaler away from heat and direct sunlight
Do not puncture or burn inhaler canister
Keep out of reach of children
